deep_dive.title: Fractal KV-Cache Archives: Lossless Symbolic Storage with In-Place Retrieval for Long-Context LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: The key-value (KV) cache dominates the memory cost of long-context autoregressive inference, and a growing body of work compresses it through quantization, eviction, or offloading. We study a complementary question: once a position's KV state has been quantized to codebook indices, how should the resulting symbol stream be stored, and can the storage layer do more than store? A family of contractive iterated-map codes that serialize a symbol sequence into a sequence of low-dimensional real vectors is revisited, and it is shown that they form a natural archive format for a quantized KV cache with the following features. The method provides exactly the access pattern a growing cache requires. It is lossless, it runs in linear time, and supports O(1) random access and O(1) amortized append. A controlled study of the quantizer feeding this archive is conducted on GPT-2 with 1024-token contexts. Keeping a small exact window (4 attention sinks + 32 recent tokens) and archiving the rest, per-head residual vector quantization reduces the archived cache by 36-54x relative to an fp16 cache at a perplexity cost of 11-15%, and we quantify a sharp key/value asymmetry -- quantizing keys is roughly 4x more damaging than quantizing values, consistent with prior low-bit KV work -- and use it to allocate bits in a hybrid scheme. Finally, we show the archive is simultaneously a search index: approximate substring queries execute directly on the stored vectors, and matched context is decoded from the matched vector without ever materializing the surrounding text. We release all code; every number reproduces from a single command on a laptop CPU.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Lossless symbolic storage and in-place retrieval for KV cache
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无损符号存储与原地检索KV缓存
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无损符号存储与原地检索KV缓存
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无损符号存储与原地检索KV缓存
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Fractal KV-Cache Archives introduces lossless symbolic storage with in-place retrieval for long-context LLM inference, using contractive iterated-map codes for efficient cache management.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文推理中KV缓存占据大量内存。本文提出分形KV缓存存档，利用收缩迭代映射码将量化后的符号流序列化为低维实向量，实现无损存储和原地检索。该方法提供精确的访问模式，支持缓存增长，且无需额外内存开销，为长上下文推理的缓存管理提供了新的高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长上下文推理中KV缓存占据大量内存。本文提出分形KV缓存存档，利用收缩迭代映射码将量化后的符号流序列化为低维实向量，实现无损存储和原地检索。该方法提供精确的访问模式，支持缓存增长，且无需额外内存开销，为长上下文推理的缓存管理提供了新的高效方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: context caching for long context LLM inference

deep_dive.title: TriRoute: Unified Learned Routing for Joint Adaptive Attention, Experts, and KV-Cache Allocation
deep_dive.abstract: Conditional computation can decouple language model quality from per-token inference cost, yet leading techniques act on a single axis in isolation: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) sparsifies the FFN, Mixture-of-Depths (MoD) skips whole transformer blocks, and KV-cache quantization compresses attention memory. We argue these three decisions (attention resolution, expert selection, and cache bit-width) are strongly coupled and should be made jointly: a token rare enough to warrant full attention may also need high-precision caching regardless of which expert processes it. We introduce TriRoute, a single lightweight controller shared across all three axes that, for every token at every layer, emits a coordinated policy: (i) an attention mode (skip/local/full), (ii) a sparse set of FFN experts (with a null expert recovering MoD), and (iii) a KV-cache bit-width. The controller trains end-to-end via a heterogeneous relaxation (Gumbel-Softmax with straight-through estimation for categorical decisions and load-balanced top-k gating for experts) under a Lagrangian budget constraint that turns the average compute and memory cost into a controllable knob. We identify a cross-axis routing-collapse cascade in naive joint training, where collapse on one axis propagates to the others, and address it with per-axis normalization and a coupling-aware balancing loss. On decoder-only models from 160M to 1.3B parameters at compute-optimal token counts, TriRoute Pareto-dominates the best independent MoD+MoE+KV-quantization combination at matched inference FLOPs and memory, while better preserving tail-case robustness on rare entities, code, and arithmetic that pure perplexity optimization erodes. Post-hoc analysis reveals interpretable structure: the controller allocates full attention and high-precision cache to sentence-initial positions, rare subwords, and named entities, while cheaply routing function words.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: joint adaptive KV-cache allocation with attention and expert routing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联合自适应KV缓存分配与注意力及专家路由
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联合自适应KV缓存分配与注意力及专家路由
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联合自适应KV缓存分配与注意力及专家路由
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TriRoute introduces a unified learned controller that jointly decides attention resolution, expert selection, and KV-cache bit-width per token, enabling coordinated efficiency gains in LLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有条件计算方法各自独立优化注意力、专家和KV缓存，忽略了三者之间的耦合关系。本文提出TriRoute，一个轻量级联合控制器，为每层每个token协同分配注意力分辨率、专家选择以及KV缓存位宽。在多个模型上的实验表明，联合策略相比独立方法能更有效地降低推理成本，同时保持模型质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有条件计算方法各自独立优化注意力、专家和KV缓存，忽略了三者之间的耦合关系。本文提出TriRoute，一个轻量级联合控制器，为每层每个token协同分配注意力分辨率、专家选择以及KV缓存位宽。在多个模型上的实验表明，联合策略相比独立方法能更有效地降低推理成本，同时保持模型质量。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: What are the latest methods for KV cache reuse in LLM inference?

deep_dive.title: Compress the Cache, Not the Speech Embedding: KV Compression for Efficient Speech LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Speech large language models (Speech LLMs) typically encode speech into sequences far longer than text, creating a major efficiency bottleneck during autoregressive decoding. A common remedy is to compress the speech sequence at the adapter level to remove temporal redundancy before it enters the LLM; however, such early downsampling risks discarding fine-grained information that cannot be recovered. We propose SpeechKV, which applies a learned pooling to the KV cache of speech tokens inside the LLM. This design allows the LLM to fuse speech and text internally while directly accelerating decoding. Trained on 71K hours of speech data, SpeechKV compresses the speech to approximately text-level granularity yet maintains performance on par with or even slightly better than the uncompressed baseline, with relative gains of 6.6% on out-of-domain entity recognition and 2.3% on OpenASR, while delivering at least 1.49 times decoding speedup that scales with audio length.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression for speech LLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向语音LLM推理加速的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向语音LLM推理加速的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向语音LLM推理加速的KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SpeechKV applies learned pooling to the KV cache of speech tokens inside the LLM, reducing sequence length to text-level granularity while preserving performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对语音大语言模型解码效率瓶颈，提出SpeechKV方法，在LLM内部对语音token的KV缓存进行可学习的池化压缩，使语音序列压缩至文本级粒度，同时保持性能与原始模型相当，显著加速解码。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对语音大语言模型解码效率瓶颈，提出SpeechKV方法，在LLM内部对语音token的KV缓存进行可学习的池化压缩，使语音序列压缩至文本级粒度，同时保持性能与原始模型相当，显著加速解码。

deep_dive.title: The Key to Going Linear: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization
deep_dive.abstract: The quadratic cost of causal self-attention severely bottlenecks long-context transformer inference. While numerous post hoc linearization pipelines exist, it is difficult to identify which components preserve model quality. This work isolates the effect of state update design in a strict frozen-backbone regime. We show that softmax relies on key-dependent, rank-1 orthogonal projections, elucidating why delta-style networks outperform purely gated accumulation. We identify a potential source of approximation errors and introduce structural interventions, specifically sink tokens, short convolutions, and fixed-budget cache routing, which reduces the remaining gap. We scale this linearization approach across LLaMA and Qwen models up to 32B parameters, outperforming prior post hoc baselines on MMLU and matching the long-context retrieval of complex adaptive-caching frameworks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: fixed-budget cache routing for KV cache optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 固定预算缓存路由用于KV缓存优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 固定预算缓存路由用于KV缓存优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 固定预算缓存路由用于KV缓存优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper linearizes transformer attention by introducing fixed-budget cache routing and other structural interventions, reducing the quadratic cost for long-context inference. Scaled to 32B models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对因果自注意力的二次复杂度问题，通过分析状态更新设计，提出了包括固定预算缓存路由在内的结构性干预，从而实现Transformer线性化，在不显著降低模型质量的情况下加速长上下文推理，并在32B规模模型上验证了有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对因果自注意力的二次复杂度问题，通过分析状态更新设计，提出了包括固定预算缓存路由在内的结构性干预，从而实现Transformer线性化，在不显著降低模型质量的情况下加速长上下文推理，并在32B规模模型上验证了有效性。

quick_skim.title: TF-Engram: A Train-Free Engram with SSD-Backed Memory for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) store factual knowledge and domain-specific patterns implicitly in dense Transformer parameters, making knowledge expansion costly through pretraining, fine-tuning, retrieval augmentation, or longer contexts. Engram-style memory offers a compact hidden-state injection pathway, but existing GPU-resident designs often rely on hash-based compression, causing unrelated phrases to collide in shared slots and weakening phrase-level semantic fidelity. We present TF-Engram, a train-free Engram system that constructs phrase-specific semantic memory offline from external corpora, stores large memory tables across a GPU--DRAM--SSD hierarchy, and uses Early-Exit Guided Predictive Prefetching to hide external-memory latency during autoregressive decoding. On Qwen3-0.6B, TF-Engram improves the average downstream score from 57.6 to 59.4, outperforming both the frozen backbone and a parameter-matched LoRA baseline. System evaluation shows that large TF-Engram tables can be built with moderate offline cost, SSD-backed storage substantially reduces GPU memory demand, and predictive prefetching recovers much of the throughput loss caused by external memory access. These results demonstrate that static phrase memory can be integrated into LLM inference as a scalable, train-free, and low-overhead system component.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: train-free semantic memory with SSD-backed caching for LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM的无训练语义记忆与SSD缓存
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向LLM的无训练语义记忆与SSD缓存
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM的无训练语义记忆与SSD缓存
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TF-Engram constructs phrase-specific semantic memory offline, stores it across GPU-DRAM-SSD hierarchy, and uses early-exit guided prefetching to accelerate LLM knowledge access without training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型知识扩展成本高昂。Engram记忆提供紧凑隐藏状态注入，但现有方法依赖哈希压缩导致语义冲突。本文提出TF-Engram，从外部语料离线构建短语级语义记忆，并存储在GPU-DRAM-SSD分层结构中，通过早期退出引导的预测预取隐藏外部存储延迟。在问答任务中，TF-Engram显著加速了知识访问，且无需训练。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大语言模型知识扩展成本高昂。Engram记忆提供紧凑隐藏状态注入，但现有方法依赖哈希压缩导致语义冲突。本文提出TF-Engram，从外部语料离线构建短语级语义记忆，并存储在GPU-DRAM-SSD分层结构中，通过早期退出引导的预测预取隐藏外部存储延迟。在问答任务中，TF-Engram显著加速了知识访问，且无需训练。
